preamble: Fortition of the lateral approximant [l] to a lateral fricative [ɬ] is a distinctive process in Northwest Caucasian languages (Abkhaz, Circassian) where oral laterals undergo strengthening to fricative articulations. This represents a major consonantal contrast maintenance strategy in these languages, which feature complex ejective and lateral-fricative inventories.

examples.note: Abkhaz demonstrates fortition of laterals within its complex consonant inventory (58 consonants in Abzhywa dialect). Lateral fricatives are contrastive.
Lateral fricatives represent a distinct phonological category in Circassian consonantal systems alongside ejectves and other complex obstruents.

sources: Beguš, G. (2021). Segmental Phonetics and Phonology in Caucasian Languages. In S. Polinsky (Ed.), The Oxford Handbook of Caucasian Languages.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
